zh.Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Bretagne,: 法國布列塔尼分尼斯艾沙吉波爾-德-萊
zh.Guilliers, Morbihan, Bretagne, France: 法國布列塔尼莫比韓吉利爾斯
zh.Josselin, Morbihan, Bretagne, France: 法國布列塔尼莫比韓越瑟林
zh.lifeSummary: 當Emma de Leon於1056年出生在法國布列塔尼分尼斯艾沙吉波爾-德-萊時，她的父親Guiomarch II DE LEON-DU QUEMENET15歲，母親Orwen de Cornouaille14歲。她於1085年與Eudon ou Eudes I Vicomte de Porhoet et Rohan在法國布列塔尼莫比韓吉利爾斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1092年在法國布列塔尼莫比韓越瑟林享年36歲，埋葬在法國布列塔尼莫比韓越瑟林。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Emma de Leon于1056年出生在法国布列塔尼分尼斯艾沙吉波爾-德-萊时，她的父亲Guiomarch II DE LEON-DU QUEMENET15岁，母亲Orwen de Cornouaille14岁。她于1085年与Eudon ou Eudes I Vicomte de Porhoet et Rohan在法国布列塔尼莫比韓吉利爾斯结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1092年在法国布列塔尼莫比韓越瑟林享年36岁，埋葬在法国布列塔尼莫比韓越瑟林。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Leon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Pedro, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Raul.

Spanish (León): habitational name from León, a city in northwestern Spain, named with Latin legio, genitive legionis ‘legion’, a division of the Roman army. In Roman times the city was the garrison of the 7th Legion, known as the Legio Gemina. The city's name became shortened from Legion(em) to Leon(em), and in this form developed an unetymological association with the word for ‘lion’ (Spanish león).

French (Léon) and Spanish (León): from the personal name Léon, León, from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn. The name Leo(n) was borne by numerous early Christian martyrs and thirteen popes.

variants.name: Len
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Jozef, Karol.
Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a flax grower or merchant, from Polish len ‘flax’.
Polish (Leń): nickname from leń ‘lazybones’.

variants.name: Leonides
meanings: Hispanic (Leónides; Mexico): from the personal name Leónides, from Greek Leonidēs, itself most likely a patronymic derivative of Leōn (from leōn ‘lion’; see Leon ) + the suffix -(e)ides.

variants.name: Lyon
meanings: Scottish, English, and French: from the personal name Middle English Lyon, Old French Leon (from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn; see Leon ). Compare Lyall .
Scottish and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lyons-la-Forêt in Eure, Normandy. It is unlikely to be from the better-known southern French city of Lyon (see 5 below).

English and French: nickname from Middle English lioun ‘lion’ (Old English, Latin leo), Old French leon, perhaps applied to a brave, fierce, or proud person, or one with a shaggy mane of hair. Compare Lion .

variants.name: Leos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Manuel, Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Santos, Jesus, Pablo, Raul, Alfonso.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Basque Leoz, itself a Castilianized form (also Léoz) of a habitational name from Leotz (in Castilian: Leoz) in Navarre.
Greek: from the personal name Leos, a pet form of Leon .

variants.name: Liang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Jian, Feng, Wen, Wei, Ping, Ying, Cheng, Guang, Jin, Jing, Liang, Chang, Chung, Hu, Nam, Pang, Jang, Min, Neng, Shen, Shiu, Sieu, Yeong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 梁, meaning ‘roofbeam’ or ‘bridge’ in Chinese: (i) from Liang (梁), the name of a state (located in present-day Hancheng in Shaanxi Province) which was granted to Kang, the youngest son of Qinzhong (died 822  BC ), because the latter made great contributions to the fight against minority ethnic groups in western China during the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After the state was annihilated by the state of Qin in 641  BC , some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from the second element of the placenames Jie Liang (解梁), Gao Liang (高粱), and Qu Liang (曲梁), names of several cities originally within the border of the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province). In 645  BC , the Duke Hui of Jin (ruler of the state of Jin, died 637  BC ) ceded these cities to the state of Qin (located originally in present-day western Shaanxi province). Later, people from these cities adopted 粱 as their surname. (iii) from Liang (梁), also known as South Liang, the name of another state (located in present-day Longxi in Gansu Province) which was granted to Tang, son of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After this state was annihilated by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the Ba Lie Lan (拔列蘭) family, members of an ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surname to 梁 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Leonti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Salvatore.
Italian (Sicily): from the Greek personal name or nickname Leon, Leontos ‘lion’ (equivalent of Latin Leo, Leonis; see Leon 2).

variants.name: Lew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Yuen, Fook, Jung, Ying, Young, Yung, Bin, Foon, Hong, Kam, Koon.
Polish: from the personal name Lew ‘lion’, adopted as a translation of Leon .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Lev  .

nameMeanings.name: Emma
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, originally a short form of compound names such as Ermintrude containing the word erm(en), irm(en) ‘entire’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, but its popularity in medieval England was greatly enhanced by the fact that it had been borne by the mother of Edward the Confessor, herself a Norman. In modern times, it was only in moderate use early in the 20th century but rose sharply in favour in the 1970s and has since remained perennially popular.

en.lifeSummary: When Emma de Leon was born in 1056, in Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Brittany, France, her father, Guiomarch II DE LEON-DU QUEMENET, was 15 and her mother, Orwen de Cornouaille, was 14. She married Eudon ou Eudes I Vicomte de Porhoet et Rohan in 1085, in Guilliers, Morbihan, Brittany, France. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1092, in Josselin, Morbihan, Brittany, France, at the age of 36, and was buried in Josselin, Morbihan, Brittany, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Emma de Leon est née en 1056, à Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Bretagne, France, son père, Guiomarch II DE LEON-DU QUEMENET, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Orwen de Cornouaille, avait 14 ans. Elle a épousé Eudon ou Eudes I Vicomte de Porhoet et Rohan en 1085, à Guilliers, Morbihan, Bretagne, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1092, à Josselin, Morbihan, Bretagne, France, à l’âge de 36 ans, et a été enterrée à Josselin, Morbihan, Bretagne, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Emma de Leon 1056 in Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Bretagne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Guiomarch II DE LEON-DU QUEMENET 15 und ihre Mutter Orwen de Cornouaille 14. Sie hat 1085 in Guilliers, Morbihan, Bretagne, Frankreich Eudon ou Eudes I Vicomte de Porhoet et Rohan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1092 im Alter von 36 Jahren in Josselin, Morbihan, Bretagne, Frankreich und wurde in Josselin, Morbihan, Bretagne, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Emma de Leon è nata il nel 1056 nel Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Bretagne, Francia, sua padre, Guiomarch II DE LEON-DU QUEMENET, aveva 15 anni e sua madre, Orwen de Cornouaille, aveva 14 anni. Lei sposato Eudon ou Eudes I Vicomte de Porhoet et Rohan nel 1085, nel Guilliers, Morbihan, Bretagne, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1092, nel Josselin, Morbihan, Bretagne, Francia, all'età di 36 anni, e fu sepolta a Josselin, Morbihan, Bretagne, Francia.

ja.Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Bretagne,: フランスブレタグンフィニストeアサイント-プオル-デ-ルeオン

ja.Josselin, Morbihan, Bretagne, France: フランスブレタグンモービハンジョセリン
ja.Normandy, France: フランスノーマンディ
ja.lifeSummary: Emma de Leonが1056年にフランスブレタグンフィニストeアサイント-プオル-デ-ルeオンで生まれた時，父Guiomarch II DE LEON-DU QUEMENETは15歳，母Orwen de Cornouailleは14歳であった。1085年にフランスブレタグンモービハングイリールズでEudon ou Eudes I Vicomte de Porhoet et Rohanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1092年にフランスブレタグンモービハンジョセリンで36歳で死去し，フランスブレタグンモービハンジョセリンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Emma de Leon(이)가 1056년 프랑스 브레타그니 핑니스트e르 세인트-포-데-e에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Guiomarch II DE LEON-DU QUEMENET(은)는 15세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Orwen de Cornouaille(은)는 14세였다. 그녀는 1085년 프랑스 브레타그니 모르비핸 길리어스에서 Eudon ou Eudes I Vicomte de Porhoet et Rohan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1092년 프랑스 브레타그니 모르비핸 조셀인에서 36세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 브레타그니 모르비핸 조셀인에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Emma de Leon nasceu em 1056, em Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Bretagne, França, seu pai, Guiomarch II DE LEON-DU QUEMENET, tinha 15 anos e sua mãe, Orwen de Cornouaille, tinha 14 anos. Ela casou-se com Eudon ou Eudes I Vicomte de Porhoet et Rohan em 1085, em Guilliers, Morbihan, Bretagne, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1092, em Josselin, Morbihan, Bretagne, França, com 36 anos, e foi sepultada em Josselin, Morbihan, Bretagne, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Emma de Leon родилась в 1056 г. в местности сеинт-пол-де-лeон, финистeр, бретагн, Франция, её отцу по имени Guiomarch II DE LEON-DU QUEMENET было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Orwen de Cornouaille, 14. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Eudon ou Eudes I Vicomte de Porhoet et Rohan в 1085 г. в местности гуиллиирс, морбихань, бретагн, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1092 г. в местности джосселинь, морбихань, бретагн, Франция в возрасте 36 лет и была похоронена в местности джосселинь, морбихань, бретагн, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Emma de Leon nació en 1056, en Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Bretagne, Francia, su papá, Guiomarch II DE LEON-DU QUEMENET, tenía 15 años y su mamá, Orwen de Cornouaille, tenía 14 años. Se casó con Eudon ou Eudes I Vicomte de Porhoet et Rohan en 1085, en Guilliers, Morbihan, Bretagne, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1092, en Josselin, Morbihan, Bretagne, Francia, a la edad de 36 años, y fue sepultada en Josselin, Morbihan, Bretagne, Francia.
